verdict_reasoning: VEGFA is a secreted growth factor with no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or covalent membrane-anchoring mechanism. Although higher-affinity heparin-binding isoforms (VEGF165, VEGF189, VEGF206) can associate with cell-surface and extracellular heparan sulfate proteoglycans, this is reversible non-covalent binding to extracellular receptors, not a stable membrane anchor of the protein itself. The protein body remains in equilibrium with the soluble pool and is recruited to the surface only via HSPG-mediated capture—a prototypical case of a secreted ligand transiently bound by surface receptors, not a surface-accessible protein by its own mechanism. No documented cell-state-induced ecto-pool, tissue-restricted surface display, lysosomal-exocytosis stage, stable TM-partner tethering, or dual-localization mechanism with a documented PM anchor.
